GitCredentialDialog.title: Authentication Required
GitCredentialDialog.description: The remote server requires credentials. Enter your username and password (or personal access token).
GitCredentialDialog.passwordHint: For non-GitHub servers, enter your username and password.
GitCredentialDialog.githubDescription: Enter a personal access token to authenticate with GitHub. The token will be validated and saved to your accounts.
GitCredentialDialog.githubTokenHint: Generate a token at GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens.

AppearanceSettings.sectionDescription: Choose light, dark, or follow system. Settings are saved automatically.

themeColor.sectionDescription: Pick a color palette for accents, buttons, and highlights.

zoomLevel.sectionDescription: Scale the entire interface. Applies immediately and persists per device.

fonts.sectionDescription: Choose fonts for the interface, code editor, and terminal. Bundled fonts load on demand; pick “Custom…” to use any font installed on your system.
fonts.terminalLigaturesHint: Terminal ligatures apply only to bundled coding fonts.

SystemSettings.sectionDescription: Manage network proxy, app updates and language preferences.
SystemSettings.proxyDescription: When enabled, subsequent network requests prefer this proxy (including ACP chat, agent installation and Git remote operations).
SystemSettings.proxyHint: Supports http(s)/socks5, example: {example}. Only effective when system proxy is enabled.
SystemSettings.proxyRequired: Proxy URL is required when proxy is enabled
SystemSettings.languageDescription: Set app language. When following system locale, unsupported languages fall back to English.
SystemSettings.updateDescription: Check the configured release source for newer versions and install directly when available.
SystemSettings.restartTimeout: The server didn't come back in time. Check the container or service logs.
SystemSettings.waitingForServer: Waiting for the server to come back...
SystemSettings.dockerUpgradeHint: This upgrades the running container now. It's lost if the container is recreated — to keep it, pull or build an image at the new version and recreate.
SystemSettings.upgradeRolledBack: Upgrade failed; the server rolled back to the previous version.
SystemSettings.serverUnreachable: Couldn't reach the server to start the upgrade. Check that it's running and try again.
SystemSettings.rollbackDescription: Restore the version installed before the last upgrade.
SystemSettings.rollbackConfirmTitle: Roll back to the previous version?
SystemSettings.rollbackConfirmDescription: The server will restart on the version installed before the last upgrade. A newer release stays available to install again.
SystemSettings.rollbackSuccess: Rolled back to the previous version. Reloading...
SystemSettings.rollbackFailed: Rollback failed. Check the server logs.

updateErrors.sourceUnavailable: Cannot reach the update source. Check your network or proxy and try again.
updateErrors.network: Network connection failed. Check your network or proxy and try again.
updateErrors.downloadFailed: Failed to download update package. Please try again later.
updateErrors.installFailed: Failed to install update. Please close the app and try again.
updateErrors.unknown: Update failed. Please try again later.

VersionControlSettings.sectionDescription: Configure Git executable and manage GitHub accounts.
VersionControlSettings.gitDescription: Configure the Git executable used by the application.
VersionControlSettings.gitNotFound: Git not found on this system
VersionControlSettings.customGitPathHint: Leave empty to use the auto-detected path.
VersionControlSettings.githubDescription: Manage GitHub accounts for authentication. Tokens are stored locally.
VersionControlSettings.tokenHint: Generate a token at GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens.
VersionControlSettings.removeConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to remove the account "{username}"?

gitAccount.sectionDescription: Manage credentials for non-GitHub Git servers (GitLab, Bitbucket, self-hosted, etc.).
gitAccount.addDescription: Enter the server address, username, and password or access token.
gitAccount.passwordHint: Enter your password or a personal access token for the server.

ShortcutSettings.recordInstruction: Click the right-side button, then press a key combination. Use Ctrl/Cmd, Alt, and Shift. Press Esc to cancel recording.

toggle_search.title: Open Search
toggle_search.description: Show or hide the conversation search panel

toggle_sidebar.title: Toggle Left Sidebar
toggle_sidebar.description: Show or hide the conversation list sidebar

toggle_terminal.title: Toggle Terminal
toggle_terminal.description: Show or hide the bottom terminal panel

close_current_terminal_tab.title: Close Current Terminal
close_current_terminal_tab.description: Close the current terminal tab when focus is on terminal

toggle_aux_panel.title: Toggle Right Panel
toggle_aux_panel.description: Show or hide the auxiliary info panel

new_conversation.title: New Conversation
new_conversation.description: Create a new conversation tab in current folder

open_folder.title: Open Folder
open_folder.description: Open folder picker and open in a new window

close_current_tab.title: Close Current Tab
close_current_tab.description: Close current conversation or file tab

close_all_file_tabs.title: Close All File Tabs
close_all_file_tabs.description: Close all open file tabs when the file pane is active

next_tab.title: Next Tab

prev_tab.title: Previous Tab
prev_tab.description: Switch to the previous conversation or file tab

send_message.title: Send Message
send_message.description: Send the current message in the input box

newline_in_message.title: Newline in Message
newline_in_message.description: Insert a newline in the message input box

SkillsSettings.title: Skills
SkillsSettings.description: Select a skill on the left. The right side previews Markdown by default; switch to edit to modify and save.
SkillsSettings.emptyNoManageableAgents: No agents available for Skills management.
SkillsSettings.agentNotSupported: Current agent does not support Skills management.
SkillsSettings.emptySkills: No skills yet. Click "New Skill" to create one.
SkillsSettings.skillsDirectoryNeedId: Skills directory: enter Skill ID to generate full path
SkillsSettings.onlyYamlMetadata: This skill only contains YAML metadata.
SkillsSettings.emptyContentHint: No content yet. Click "Edit" to start.
SkillsSettings.noSelectionHint: Select a skill on the left, or click "New Skill" to create one.

scope.pickFolderHint: Select a folder to view its skills.

templates.gemini:
---
name: example-skill
description: Describe when this skill should be used.
---

# Skill Name

Instructions for the agent when this skill is active.

## Workflow

1. Add actionable step one.
2. Add actionable step two.
templates.openCode:
---
name: example-skill
description: Describe when this skill should be used.
---

# Purpose

Describe what this skill helps with.

# Steps

1. Add actionable step one.
2. Add actionable step two.
templates.openClaw:
---
name: example-skill
description: Describe when this skill should be used.
user-invocable: true
disable-model-invocation: false
---

# Purpose

Describe what this skill helps with.

# Instructions

1. Add actionable instruction one.
2. Add actionable instruction two.
templates.default:
---
name: example-skill
description: Describe when this skill should be used.
---

# Skill: example-skill

## When to use

- Describe trigger conditions.

## Instructions

1. Add actionable instruction one.
2. Add actionable instruction two.

errors.fieldOutOfRange: {field} value is out of allowed range
errors.specMustBeObject: MCP config must be a JSON object.
errors.missingType: MCP config is missing the type field. Use one of stdio, http (aliases: streamable-http, streamableHttp), sse.
errors.unsupportedType: Unsupported MCP type {type}. Supported: stdio, http (aliases: streamable-http, streamableHttp), sse.
errors.codexEntryUnsupportedType: Codex MCP entry {id} has unsupported type {type}. Supported: stdio, http (aliases: streamable-http, streamableHttp), sse.
errors.unsupportedTransportType: Unsupported transport type {type}. Supported: http (aliases: streamable-http, streamableHttp), sse.
errors.stdioCommandRequired: stdio MCP requires a non-empty command field.
errors.remoteUrlRequired: Remote MCP requires a non-empty url field.
errors.appsRequired: Select at least one target app.

toasts.selectAtLeastOneApp: Please select at least one target app
toasts.serverIdExists: Server ID "{id}" already exists. Edit the existing entry or pick a different name.

local.description: Local MCP configuration can be edited and saved directly.
local.draftDescription: Provide a Server ID and configuration to create a local MCP server.
local.typeHint: Supported types: stdio, http (aliases: streamable-http, streamableHttp), sse. env is only used by stdio; for remote MCPs use headers to carry auth tokens.
local.envOnRemoteWarning: env detected on a remote MCP. Only stdio uses env; remote MCPs carry auth tokens via headers, so env will be ignored on save.

market.installConfigDescription: Install Config (JSON, editable before install; edits will override protocol/parameter form)
market.selectLeftToView: Select a marketplace MCP on the left to view details.

McpSettings.selectLeftMcp: Select an MCP on the left.

AcpAgentSettings.title: Agent SDK Management
AcpAgentSettings.description: Manage Agent SDK connection, enabled state, environment variables, config management and version preflight info in one place.
AcpAgentSettings.modelHintDefault: Leave empty to use system default model.
AcpAgentSettings.generalConfigDescriptionClaude: Supports quick configuration for API URL, API Key and Claude models, and syncs with native JSON config.
AcpAgentSettings.generalConfigDescriptionDefault: Supports important config input (API URL, API Key, Model) and native JSON config management.

multiAgent.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration
multiAgent.description: Allow active agents to delegate sub-tasks to other agents.
multiAgent.enableHint: When off, the delegate_to_agent tool is hidden from the agent's MCP catalog.
multiAgent.depthHint: Allowed range: {min}–{max}. Caps how deep a chain (root → child → grandchild …) can recurse.
multiAgent.completedCacheHint: In-memory cache of finished sub-agent results, held only while the delegating session is running and cleared automatically when it ends. Over this budget the oldest results are dropped from memory first (still viewable in the sub-agent's own session); 0 = unlimited (still cleared when the session ends).
multiAgent.agentDefaultsDescription: Per-agent overrides applied when Multi-Agent Collaboration spawns a subagent for a delegation call. Options shown here come from a live probe — what you pick is exactly what the agent will accept.
multiAgent.probing: Loading available options from the agent…
multiAgent.noConfigAvailable: This agent has no configurable options.

codex.configDescription: Supports quick configuration for API URL, API Key, model name and reasoning effort, and syncs with `auth.json` / `config.toml`.
codex.chatgptSubscriptionHint: Log in with ChatGPT official subscription, no API Key required
codex.apiKeyHint: Connect using API Key to OpenAI or compatible API services
codex.enableFastAria: Enable Fast service tier for Codex
codex.loginStep1: Open the following URL in your browser:
codex.loginTimeout: Login timed out, please try again
codex.loginSaveFailed: Login succeeded but failed to save config

gemini.authConfigDescription: Aligned with Gemini CLI authentication docs, supporting custom endpoint, Google login, Gemini API Key and Vertex AI (ADC / service account / API Key).

hint.custom: Fill API URL, API Key and Model, mapped to GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL / GEMINI_API_KEY / GEMINI_MODEL.
hint.loginGoogle: Run gemini in terminal and complete Google login first; API key is not required.
hint.geminiApiKey: Fill GEMINI_API_KEY when using Gemini API.
hint.vertexAdc: Use gcloud ADC; GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT and GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION are recommended.
hint.vertexServiceAccount: Set service account JSON path to GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.
hint.vertexApiKey: Fill GOOGLE_API_KEY when using Vertex AI API key.

openCode.configDescription: Aligned with OpenCode `provider` schema, supports multi-provider management and two-way sync with native JSON files.
openCode.emptyProvider: No provider yet. Enter an ID then click "Add Provider".
openCode.modelDescription: Aligned with OpenCode `provider.models`. Fast management currently supports `name` / `id`; other advanced fields are preserved and can be edited in native JSON below.
openCode.emptyModel: No model yet. Enter model id then click "Add model".

openClaw.gatewayDescription: Configure OpenClaw Gateway connection. Supports local or remote gateway.
openClaw.gatewayUrlHint: Leave empty to use gateway.remote.url from local openclaw config.
openClaw.gatewayTokenHint: Use token-file instead of plain token when possible; configure via openclaw CLI.
openClaw.sessionKeyHint: Optional. Specify gateway session key; leave empty to auto-assign isolated session.

hermes.configDescription: Hermes manages its own credentials in ~/.hermes/.env and settings in ~/.hermes/config.yaml. Pick a provider, then set the API key and model — codeg writes both files for you.
hermes.providerHint: The provider selects which API key variable and model.provider Hermes uses. OAuth providers are configured through the terminal setup below.
hermes.apiKeyHint: Saved to ~/.hermes/.env. It is never injected into the process — Hermes reads it from its own config.
hermes.oauthHint: This provider uses OAuth. Run the setup below to authenticate in your terminal.
hermes.awsHint: Bedrock uses your AWS credentials (environment or shared config). Set the model id above and configure AWS access in your environment.
hermes.unsupportedProvider: This provider isn't editable with structured fields. Use the raw config.yaml editor below or the terminal setup.
hermes.setupHint: Hermes's interactive setup needs a terminal. Launch it below, or copy the command to run it yourself.
hermes.rawConfigHint: Edit ~/.hermes/config.yaml directly. Saving here overwrites the file verbatim.

codebuddy.configDescription: CodeBuddy authenticates with an API key. Mainland China builds also require the environment set to “China (internal)”; iOA builds use “iOA”. The overseas build leaves it unset.
codebuddy.apiKeyHint: Saved as CODEBUDDY_API_KEY for this agent. Alternatively, sign in with the CodeBuddy CLI in a terminal.
codebuddy.apiKeyHintSelfHosted: Saved as CODEBUDDY_API_KEY for this agent. Use the key issued by your private deployment.
codebuddy.environmentHint: Sets CODEBUDDY_INTERNET_ENVIRONMENT. Mainland China must use “China (internal)”; the overseas build leaves it unset.
codebuddy.baseUrlHint: Saved as CODEBUDDY_BASE_URL and points CodeBuddy at your private endpoint. The region setting is left unset for self-hosted deployments.
codebuddy.loginHint: No API key? Run “codebuddy” in a terminal to sign in with your Tencent account instead.

kimiCode.configDescription: `kimi acp` only authenticates with a stored login token and rejects API keys on their own. To use an API key, codeg writes a managed ~/.kimi-code/config.toml provider AND seeds a local gate token so the session opens — inference still runs on your key.
kimiCode.gateReadyApiKey: Authenticated via API key (codeg seeded the local gate token).
kimiCode.gateReadyLogin: Authenticated via a Kimi account login.
kimiCode.gateNotReady: Not authenticated yet — save an API key below (or sign in) to open Kimi sessions.
kimiCode.authModeHint: “API key” writes a codeg-managed config.toml provider and seeds a gate token. “Login” clears that and uses a real `kimi login` session (requires a Kimi subscription).
kimiCode.interfaceTypeHint: The provider protocol Kimi speaks (config.toml `type`). Use Kimi / Moonshot for a Moonshot / platform.kimi.com key.
kimiCode.endpointHint: International = api.moonshot.ai; China (platform.kimi.com keys) = api.moonshot.cn. Custom points at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
kimiCode.baseUrlHint: Leave blank to use the provider SDK default.
kimiCode.apiKeyHint: Written to ~/.kimi-code/config.toml and used for inference. From platform.kimi.com or platform.kimi.ai.
kimiCode.authTypeHint: Where the API key is written inside config.toml.
kimiCode.vertexHint: Vertex AI uses Google Application Default Credentials — run `gcloud auth application-default login` (no API key).
kimiCode.modelHint: The model id to use. Click Fetch to list the models your key can access (e.g. kimi-k2.7-code) — avoids “model not found” errors.
kimiCode.fetchModelsEmpty: Key works, but no models were returned
kimiCode.fetchModelsFailed: Couldn’t list models — check the key and endpoint
kimiCode.fetchModelsNeedsKey: Enter an API key and endpoint first
kimiCode.maxContextHint: Tokens, e.g. 262144. Leave blank to use the model default.
kimiCode.loginHint: Run `kimi login` in a terminal to sign in with a Kimi subscription account (device-code OAuth). codeg stores nothing and reuses Kimi’s own login. Saving here removes codeg’s API-key gate token.
kimiCode.rawEditorHint: Written verbatim (comments preserved) and validated as TOML. codeg also seeds the gate token so the session opens.

AcpAgentSettings.modelProviderHint: Use API URL, API Key, and model from a configured model provider.
AcpAgentSettings.noModelProviderAvailable: No model provider configured for this agent. Go to Model Provider Settings to add one.

claude.officialSubscriptionHint: Use official Anthropic subscription, no API Key required.
claude.customModelOptionHint: Adds a single custom entry to Claude's model picker (e.g. a model behind a custom gateway/proxy). Name and description are optional display overrides.

toasts.createdAndSwitchedNewBranch: Created and switched to new branch

dialogs.newBranchDescription: Create a new branch with commit {shortHash} as the latest commit.

soft.label: --soft
soft.description:
Move HEAD and the current branch pointer to the target commit.
Keep Index and Working Tree unchanged.
Changes from the removed commits stay staged.

mixed.label: --mixed (default)
mixed.description:
Move HEAD to the target commit.
Reset Index to the target commit while keeping Working Tree changes.
Those changes become unstaged.

hard.label: --hard
hard.description:
Move HEAD and reset both Index and Working Tree to the target commit.
Local tracked changes after the target commit are discarded.
This is a destructive operation.

keep.label: --keep
keep.description:
Move HEAD to the target commit and keep local changes when possible.
Only non-conflicting local changes are preserved.
If conflicts are detected, reset aborts to protect your work.

tasks.loadingSelectorsDescription: Fetching mode and session config options

messageThread.emptyDescription: Start a conversation to see messages here

messageInput.pasteUnavailable: Couldn't read the clipboard. Press Ctrl/⌘V to paste instead.
messageInput.clipboardWriteFailed: Couldn't write to the clipboard. Use the keyboard shortcut instead.
messageInput.liveFeedbackDisabledHint: Available while the agent is working
messageInput.expertsEmptyForAgent: This agent has no enabled experts. Enable them in Settings > Experts.
messageInput.attachUploadTooLarge: {names} exceeds the {limit}MB upload limit and was skipped.
messageInput.attachUploadNotAFile: {names} isn't a regular file (directory or special file) and was skipped.
messageInput.attachUploadQuotaExceeded: The server is out of upload storage; {names} couldn't be uploaded.
messageInput.mentionMore: More results — keep typing to filter

welcomeInputPanel.autoConnectFallback: Click to open {path} and manage installation.
welcomeInputPanel.autoConnectAppend: {message}. Click to open {path} and manage installation.
welcomeInputPanel.enableAgentFirstPlaceholder: Enable at least one agent before starting a session...
welcomeInputPanel.prepareSessionFailed: Couldn't prepare the chat session. Please try again.
welcomeInputPanel.createConversationFailed: Couldn't create the conversation. Please try again.

welcomePanel.greeting: What would you like to <highlight>do today</highlight>?

tips.tileTabs: Right-click any conversation tab and choose Tile Display to see multiple sessions side by side.
tips.pinTab: Double-click a conversation tab to pin it, so a newly opened session won't auto-replace it.
tips.shortcutsNewSearch: <shortcut>{newConversation}</shortcut> opens a new conversation, <shortcut>{searchConversations}</shortcut> searches your history.
tips.slashAtMention: Type / in the input to trigger slash commands, or @ to reference a file from the project.
tips.pasteDropFiles: Paste a screenshot or drop a file directly onto the input to attach it.
tips.queueMessage: While the agent is replying, keep typing — your next message gets queued and auto-sent when it finishes.
tips.draftAutoSave: Unsent drafts are auto-saved per conversation and restored when you come back.
tips.forkSend: The send button's dropdown has Fork and Send — branch from the current turn into a new tab.
tips.exportConversation: Right-click inside the conversation to export it as Markdown, HTML, or an image.
tips.chatChannels: Connect Telegram / Lark / WeChat under Settings → Chat Channels to continue from your phone.
tips.shortcutsAuxPanel: <shortcut>{toggleAuxPanel}</shortcut> toggles the right panel to view this session's touched files and Git changes.
tips.shortcutsTerminalSidebar: <shortcut>{toggleTerminal}</shortcut> opens the built-in terminal, <shortcut>{toggleSidebar}</shortcut> toggles the sidebar.
tips.customShortcuts: Every shortcut is fully remappable under Settings → Shortcuts.
tips.webService: Enable Settings → Web Service so teammates can reach the same codeg from a browser.
tips.fusionMode: Open a file or diff to automatically show it alongside the conversation.
tips.quickMessages: Open the + button next to the input and pick Quick Messages to insert a saved snippet (manage them under Settings → Quick Messages).
tips.experts: The + button has an Expert Skills menu — preload roles like Debug or Planning with one click.

prompts.excel:
Create an Excel workbook with the following requirements:

[describe your data, tables, formulas, and charts here]
prompts.word:
Create a Word document with the following requirements:

[describe your document content, structure, and formatting here]
prompts.ppt:
Create a PowerPoint presentation with the following requirements:

[describe your slides, content, and design here]
prompts.pitchDeck:
Create a fundraising pitch deck with the following requirements:

[describe your company, funding round, and key metrics here]
prompts.morph:
Create a presentation with Morph transition animations:

[describe your presentation topic and desired visual effects here]
prompts.morph3d:
Create a 3D Morph presentation with GLB models and camera moves:

[describe your topic and 3D visual concept here]
prompts.academic:
Write an academic paper in Word with the following requirements:

[describe your research topic, methodology, and key findings here]
prompts.financial:
Create a financial model in Excel with the following requirements:

[describe your financial statements, projections, and analysis here]
prompts.dashboard:
Create a data dashboard in Excel with the following requirements:

[describe your data source, KPIs, and charts here]

hyperframes.title: HyperFrames Video Project
hyperframes.subtitle: Scaffold an HTML-to-video project. Your workspace agent can then write and render it.
hyperframes.skillsDesc: Globally install the HyperFrames skills (symlinked) for the selected agents so they can author and render video.

createDialog.title: Create Project

toasts.openWorkspaceFailed: Project created, but couldn't open it in the workspace

WebServiceSettings.addressSwitchHint: Switching only changes the address shown and opened here — the service listens on all interfaces and stays reachable at every address.
WebServiceSettings.sectionDescription: Enable to access Codeg remotely via browser
WebServiceSettings.autoStartHint: Start the Web service when Codeg launches
WebServiceSettings.saveConfigFailed: Failed to save Web service settings
WebServiceSettings.qrcodeHint: Scan with your phone to open Codeg in a browser
WebServiceSettings.tokenHint: Enter this token when accessing the Web client for the first time
WebServiceSettings.stalePortOccupiedTitle: Port {port} is held by another process
WebServiceSettings.stalePortHint: Codeg can't bind here until the port is released. Change the port above, or close the process holding it.

errors.portInUse: Port {port} is already in use. Close the process using it or choose another port.
errors.permissionDenied: Permission denied. Try a port above 1024 or run with higher privileges.
errors.addressUnavailable: The address is not available on this machine

ChatChannelSettings.sectionDescription: Configure IM bots to receive event notifications and query coding activity.
ChatChannelSettings.deleteConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete the channel and its message logs. Are you sure?
ChatChannelSettings.channelListDescription: Enabled channels will auto-connect when the service starts.
ChatChannelSettings.weixinScanDescription: Open WeChat and scan the QR code to connect.
ChatChannelSettings.weixinReconnectNotice: Due to iLink protocol limitations, after each reconnection you must send a message to the bot before event triggers take effect.

commands.title: Built-in Commands
commands.description: Bot commands available in chat channels. In group chats, @Bot is required to process messages.
commands.prefixDescription: 1-3 non-alphanumeric characters used to trigger bot commands (default /).

events.title: Event Notifications
events.description: When enabled, triggered events will be pushed to the channel.
events.errorDesc: When an agent encounters an error
events.permissionRequestDesc: When an agent requests permission to act
events.questionRequestDesc: When an agent asks you a question
events.userPromptSentDesc: When you send a message — the message text is included in the notification
events.webhooksDescription: POST a JSON payload to one or more URLs when an enabled event fires. The event filter above also applies to webhooks.
events.docsNote: Each enabled event is delivered to every URL. Webhooks are not debounced; the event filter above still applies.
events.deleteWebhookMessage: Remove this webhook? Events will no longer be delivered to this URL.

language.title: Message Language
language.description: Language used for event notifications, command responses, and daily reports sent to chat channels.

ModelProviderSettings.sectionDescription: Manage API provider credentials for agents.
ModelProviderSettings.agentTypesRequired: At least one agent type is required.
ModelProviderSettings.agentTypeImmutableHint: Agent type cannot be changed after creation.
ModelProviderSettings.claudeCustomModelOptionHint: Adds a single custom entry to Claude's model picker (e.g. a model behind a custom gateway/proxy). Name and description are optional display overrides.
ModelProviderSettings.deleteConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete the provider "{name}". Are you sure?
ModelProviderSettings.deleteBlockedByAgent: {agents} is currently using this provider. Please unlink before deleting.
ModelProviderSettings.affectedRunningSessions: {count, plural, one {# running session needs to reconnect to apply the change} other {# running sessions need to reconnect to apply the change}}

ExpertsSettings.title: Expert Skills
ExpertsSettings.description: Enable curated, battle-tested skill workflows for your AI coding agents. Each expert is a standalone skill from the superpowers project — codeg manages the central copy and links it into the agents you choose.
ExpertsSettings.emptyExperts: No experts available. Check the application logs.
ExpertsSettings.emptySelection: Select an expert to see its content and manage activation.
ExpertsSettings.emptySearch: No experts match the current search.
ExpertsSettings.searchPlaceholder: Search experts by name, id, or description
ExpertsSettings.copyModeWarning: Copied (not linked). Re-enable after codeg updates to get the latest version.

OfficeToolsSettings.description: Manage OfficeCLI skills for creating Excel, Word, and PowerPoint files. Install OfficeCLI, sync skills, and enable them per agent.

OfficeToolsSettings.installFirst: Install OfficeCLI first to enable skills for agents.
OfficeToolsSettings.syncFirst: Sync skills first to load this skill's content.

detection.installHint: Install OfficeCLI to enable office document generation skills for your AI agents.

BackupSettings.title: Backup & Restore
BackupSettings.description: Export a portable backup of your codeg data, or restore from one.

export.includeExternalHint: Also archive agent CLI transcripts (Claude, Codex, Gemini, …). Increases size.
export.passphrasePlaceholder: Leave empty for an unencrypted archive
export.noPassphraseWarning: This backup will contain secrets (API keys, tokens) in plaintext. Store it somewhere safe.
export.passphraseLossWarning: Encrypted with this passphrase. If you lose it, the backup cannot be recovered.

restore.passphrasePrompt: This backup is encrypted. Enter its passphrase.

preview.title: Backup details
preview.incompatibleHint: This backup was created by a newer version of codeg and cannot be restored.

restore.replaceWarning: Restoring replaces all current codeg data (database and uploads). Your current data is snapshotted first so it can be recovered.
restore.keyringNote: Desktop GitHub/chat tokens live in your OS keychain and are not included; re-enter them after restoring.
restore.restartTimeout: The server did not come back in time. Reload the page once it is up.
restore.confirmBody: This will replace your current codeg database and uploads with the backup, then restart. Your current data is snapshotted first.

external.title: Conversation content
external.hint: This backup includes agent CLI transcripts. Choose where to restore them.
external.modeSide: Restore to a safe side folder
external.forceOverwriteHint: Replace files that already exist in the agent CLI folders.
external.noConflicts: No existing files would be overwritten.
external.conflictSkipNote: Existing files are kept (skipped) unless you enable overwrite.

restore.restartFailed: Restore staged, but the server couldn't restart. Restart it manually to apply the restore.

BackupSettings.remoteUnsupported: Backup & restore acts on the data of the machine running codeg. You're connected to a remote workspace — manage its backups from that server directly.

error.unknownFormat: This file is not a recognized codeg backup.
error.newerVersion: This backup was created by codeg {backupVersion}, newer than this version ({appVersion}).
error.alreadyPending: A restore is already staged. Restart to apply it before staging another.

error.diskSpace: Not enough disk space to complete the operation.

WebConnection.reconnectingDescription: Trying to reconnect to the server. This usually recovers on its own within a few seconds.
WebConnection.sessionExpiredDescription: Your session is no longer valid. Please sign in again to continue.

SessionFeedbackSettings.title: Live Feedback
SessionFeedbackSettings.description: Send notes and corrections to an agent while it's working. When enabled, the agent gets a tool to check for your mid-task feedback, and conversations show a note input. Tip: agents typically only check for feedback when you mention it in your prompt — for example, add "check my live feedback regularly" to your message so the agent knows to look.
SessionFeedbackSettings.enableHint: Adds a check-for-feedback tool to agents started after this is turned on.

LiveFeedback.placeholder: Send a note to {agent} while it works…
LiveFeedback.dialogDescription: Send a note to the agent while it works. It reads your note the next time it checks — without interrupting the current step.
LiveFeedback.turnEndedUnread: The agent finished before reading your feedback.
LiveFeedback.turnEndedResent: The turn ended — sent as a new message instead.

SessionInfoSettings.title: Get session info
SessionInfoSettings.description: Let agents look up a session you reference in your message (a session badge) to read its title, agent, status, workspace, token usage, and recent messages.
SessionInfoSettings.enableHint: Adds the get_session_info tool to agents started after this is turned on.
SessionInfoSettings.saveFailed: Failed to save session info settings

AskQuestionSettings.title: Ask user question
AskQuestionSettings.description: Let agents pause and ask you a multiple-choice question that appears above the conversation input box. The agent waits until you answer (or skip).
AskQuestionSettings.enableHint: Adds the ask_user_question tool to agents started after this is turned on.

LogsSettings.sectionDescription: View and configure application diagnostic logs. Logs are written to local files and kept in memory for live viewing.
LogsSettings.captureDescription: Controls how much detail is captured. Higher levels (Debug, Trace) record more but produce larger logs. Off disables logging.
LogsSettings.viewerDescription: Live view of recent log records. Filter by level or search text.
LogsSettings.filesDescription: Download full log files from disk for history beyond the live buffer.
LogsSettings.downloadTruncated: File is large; downloaded the latest {size}. The full file is in the logs directory.
LogsSettings.captureEnvLocked: Log level is controlled by the RUST_LOG / CODEG_LOG environment variable; change it there to take effect.
LogsSettings.targetsDescription: Set a different level for specific modules (e.g. codeg_lib::acp) without changing the global level.

Automations.title: Automations
Automations.emptyHint: Create one to run an agent task on a schedule or on demand.
Automations.name: Name
Automations.isolationSharedCaveat: Runs use this folder's working tree directly — they can collide with your uncommitted changes. Enable the worktree option to isolate each run.
Automations.deleteDescription: This removes the automation and its schedule. Run history is kept.
Automations.errorCron: A cron expression is required for scheduled automations
Automations.configNone: This agent has no configurable options
Automations.selectHint: Select an automation to see details
Automations.onboardHint: Schedule an agent to review code, update dependencies, or triage issues — on a cadence or on demand.
Automations.blankDesc: Configure an agent task from scratch.
Automations.tplCodeReviewDesc: Review recent changes for bugs, regressions, and quality issues.
Automations.tplDependencyUpdatesDesc: Find outdated dependencies and propose safe upgrades.
Automations.tplTestCoverageDesc: Find untested code paths and add the missing tests.
Automations.tplTodoSweepDesc: Collect TODO and FIXME comments and triage them by priority.
Automations.tplCiTriageDesc: Investigate recent failing checks and propose fixes.
Automations.tplReleaseNotesDesc: Summarize changes since the last release into a changelog.
Automations.tplSecurityAuditDesc: Scan for vulnerabilities and risky patterns, then report findings.
